input.headline: REI’s biggest sale of the year is here — I found 51 great deals worth shopping

raw.headline: REI’s biggest sale of the year is here — I found 51 great deals worth shopping

winner: REI's biggest sale—until you realize "great deals" just means they're finally selling out their inventory of half-broken camping gear.

candidates.text: Why did the hiker bring a ladder to REI's big sale? Because they heard the deals were *on a whole other level*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'on a whole other level' are clever and specific to the context of a sale, with a natural play on words related to hiking and shopping."}

candidates.text: I told my wife about REI's big sale—she said, "Great, now we can finally buy those 51 deals." I said, "Babe, we're not building a mall out of them.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline. It uses a relatable scenario with a clever twist that ties back to the number of deals mentioned."}

candidates.text: REI's biggest *anti-sale* of the year—where you pay *extra* for nothing!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with clever wordplay and specificity."}

candidates.text: REI's biggest sale—until you realize "great deals" just means they're finally selling out their inventory of half-broken camping gear.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is specific, surprising, and ties directly to the headline with a clever twist. It’s slightly generic in its sarcasm but still effective and concise."}

candidates.text: I’m just glad REI’s sale didn’t include any of those “limited-time only” deals that disappear after three clicks—those are the real steals.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is timely and specific to the context of online shopping, with a touch of sarcasm about limited-time deals. It's slightly generic in its humor but still effective and natural."}
